_doc: Daily market-state snapshot. Written by the close-of-market run, read by the next morning run (and the next close run for day-over-day deltas). This is reasoning fuel — the reader never sees this file, only the conclusions drawn from it. Fields left null were not collected; collect them next run rather than guessing. Add fields freely as new data sources prove useful; note new sources in playbook.md.

spx.note: Closed essentially at the session low (SPY low 725.33 vs close 725.43) and within 2 points of the computed 7,265 put wall. ~4.1-4.4% off the record (NOTE: record-close reference is inconsistent in our own files — 7,575 in market_state vs 7,599.96 in the June 1 research notes; reconcile next run and use one number).

dow.note: -953.33 points; first close below 50,000 since the streak began

after_hours_events.event: ORCL Q4 FY26: rev $19.2B +21% beat, EPS $2.11 beat, RPO $553B->$638B (+$85B QoQ, almost all large-scale AI contracts), IaaS +93%. But FY26 capex $55.7B vs $50B guided, FY27 net capex outlay ~$70B, and a ~$40B FY27 debt+equity raise announced (incl $20B ATM). Stock fell as much as ~7% AH. New disclosure: $75B cumulative customer GPU prepayments.
after_hours_events.read: Demand leg record, funding leg sold. Read-through pressure on AI-infra names (CRWV, VRT, DELL) at tomorrow's open; the market is auditing balance sheets, not order books.

regime.rationale: Held, but barely — one bad close from sideways. Still above the 50-DMA (~7,180, +1.2%) and within ~4% of the record, but: soft-core CPI relief got SOLD (closed at the lows), dip-buyers were absent for the first time in three tests, net GEX deepened to -$59B/-$74B (dealers amplify), and the index closed ON the put wall. Sellers had three reasons (war, supercore, IPO supply) and buyers had one (soft core) — and the one got overrun.
regime.what_changes_it: Close below ~7,150-7,180 (price shelf + 50-DMA confluence) -> sideways or worse. Reclaim of the 7,420-7,450 flip zone -> bull confirmed (this is the live 2026-06-09-pm-gamma-reclaim call, due June 12). Hormuz closure or WTI >$95 -> bear-rally risk.

key_levels.notes: 7265 = computed put wall (both feeds), closed 2pts above it; 7150 = price shelf with 50-DMA (~7,180) just above it — confluence zone 7,150-7,180. 7387 = June 9 close / breakdown level; 7420-7450 = flip zone.

gamma.source: polygon/computed (scripts/market_levels.py --audit, verdict: pass 9/9). CBOE crosscheck: flip 7450 (0.38% from Polygon 7422 — treat flip as a 7,420-7,450 ZONE), call wall 7500 exact, put wall 7265 exact, net GEX -$59B (vs Polygon -$74B).
gamma.note: Put wall ROLLED DOWN 7300 -> 7265 overnight (fresh hedging stacked lower after the 7,300 break) and SPX closed 7,266.99 — within 2 points of it. Tape-coherence: the close pinned to the computed wall is the strongest live validation of the computed levels yet. Net GEX doubled deeper negative (-$35B -> -$59/-74B): the most amplified tape of the quarter. Near-view (<=2DTE): put shelf 7265 on BOTH feeds (no separate higher ledge tonight — the near fight and the big magnet are the same level now); near call wall DISAGREES (Polygon 7700 artifact-suspect vs CBOE 7430) — using CBOE 7430 for the chart since it coheres with the flip zone; treat overhead as 7,420-7,450 first, 7,500 above.
gamma.caveat: Polygon's reported spot (7370.5) was stale at fetch time (~18:36 ET); CBOE spot 7266.99 matched the close. Walls/flip derive from OI not spot, and both feeds agree on walls exactly — levels accepted. Watch the Polygon near-view call wall artifact (7700) again tomorrow.
gamma.audit_log: 2026-06-10 side-by-side day 2: fresh WebSearch snippet returned a STALE page (referenced June 8 close 7,405.72; flip 7,445, put wall 7,000, net GEX -$19.2B) — unusable for comparison, logged per trust-period protocol. Script's internal snippet comparison (vs June 9 stored values) passed at <=1.32%. Tape-coherence substituted for snippet check: computed put wall 7265 vs actual close 7266.99 — pass.

rates_commodities.note: 10Y EASED to ~4.52% from 4.55% intraday high — the bond market bought the soft core even as equities sold; hike bets for June 17 pared but Dec-hike odds still ~70% (morning read). WTI +2.07% settle, UP on Trump 'attacking them very hard' but still $5 under the $95 line (oil-is-the-tell intact, barely tested). Gold ~$4,165-4,200, LOWEST since March 23, below its 200-DMA for the first time since Oct 2023, 25% off the Jan ATH — gold falling on a war day reads as liquidation/deleveraging, not safe-haven rotation; worth carrying as a regime tell.

volatility_flow.vix_note: VIX +2.35 (+11.83%) from June 9's 19.87 close (which backfills yesterday's open question). 22+ with SPX pinned to the put wall and net GEX at -$59/-74B = the jumpiest configuration of the quarter.
volatility_flow.put_call_note: Not collected today (lags 1-2 days in free sources); June 5 print was 0.67.
volatility_flow.note: ORCL options implied ~12% post-earnings move (Bloomberg via Investing.com) — AH move ran ~half that.

technicals.spx_vs_50dma: +1.2% (50-DMA ~7,180 est from June 8 reading of ~7,174.5; SPX 7,266.99) — cushion nearly gone, was +3.0% yesterday
technicals.breadth_note: Broad distribution, not narrow damage: industrials -3%+, tech and materials -2%+. Contrast with June 9 (only tech and energy red). Gold below its own 200-DMA the same day = cross-asset de-risking.
technicals.note: DMAs drift slowly; June 8 values carried forward two days. The 7,150 price shelf and the ~7,180 50-DMA now form one support zone — a close below it would be the first 50-DMA loss since the streak began.

calendar.why: First software-side AI monetization read since the rout; not a holding, but the 'show me the revenue' theme transfers.

calendar.event: May PPI (date unconfirmed — verify in morning run)
calendar.why: Pipeline pressure check on the supercore question; April PPI was the original regime-shift scare.
SPX needs a close above ~7,440 by Friday or the dip-buyer thesis is graded wrong.
Soft core m/m argues hold; supercore 3.67% y/y accelerating since fall is the hawks' exhibit. Hike risk reduced, not removed.
Largest expiry of the quarter; the deep negative gamma regime unclenches (or doesn't) here.
MRVL closed 252.59 — $2.59 above the prediction's $250 falsifier; the inclusion-bid experiment is at its decision point.
Fed's preferred gauge; supercore echo check.

open_questions: ANSWERED 2026-06-10 (from June 9): VIX June 9 close was 19.87; June 10 closed ~22.22 (+11.8%).

ANSWERED 2026-06-10 (from June 9): overnight crude DID confirm the de-escalation read initially (WTI ~$88 through Iran's retaliation) — then Trump's 'attack very hard' pushed WTI +2.07% to $90.03. Still under $95: oil-is-the-tell intact but having its first real test.
ANSWERED 2026-06-10 (from June 9): SPX did NOT reclaim the flip on the tame core — it lost another 120 points. The reclaim call (7,440 by June 12) is two sessions from failing.

Does the 7,265 put wall hold a retest tomorrow? It pinned the close tonight; if it breaks with this much negative gamma, dealer hedging chases the move and 7,150-7,180 (price shelf + 50-DMA) is the next stand.

Does ORCL's -7% AH bleed into AI-infra names at the open (CRWV, VRT, DELL all have funding-narrative beta), or does the $75B customer-prepayment disclosure get re-read as bullish once the call is digested?
MRVL at 252.59 vs the 250 falsifier with the June 22 inclusion bid 8 sessions away — does the mechanical bid show up before the line breaks?

Supercore decomposition: airfares (+2.7% m/m) clearly contributed and insurance fell (-1.7%) — but what drove the rest of the 0.55%? BLS detail tables (recreation, medical m/m) would settle the benign-vs-ugly reading; check tomorrow.

Was today's gold break below the 200-DMA (first since Oct 2023) IPO-treasury-raising / deleveraging flow? If gold keeps falling while VIX rises, that's the liquidation signature worth a letter section.
